title: Battery Runtime Calculator (Ah / Hours) | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate battery runtime in hours based on amp-hour capacity and power consumption. Account for discharge efficiency and safe depth-of-discharge (DoD).
intro: Battery runtime calculators help you design reliable power systems. Whether sizing solar backup, RV electrical systems, UPS installations, or IoT devices, accurate runtime estimates prevent undersizing, unexpected downtime, and costly over-engineering. Calculate how many hours your battery will power a specific load based on capacity (Ah), voltage, and safe discharge limits. Values updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly.
keyTakeaway: **t = (Ah × V × η × DoD) / P_consumption**. For a 100Ah/12V battery at 80% DoD with 100W load → 9.6 hours. Lead-acid: DoD ≤ 50%. LiFePO4: DoD ≤ 80%.

example.result: A 100Ah 12V battery powers a 100W load for 9.1 hours

explanation:
Battery runtime calculators are essential for designing reliable power systems. Whether planning solar backup, RV electrical systems, UPS sizing, or IoT device power budgets, knowing your battery runtime prevents undersizing, unexpected downtime, and costly over-engineering.

## How It's Calculated

Battery runtime depends on four key factors: amp-hour capacity (Ah), voltage (V), power consumption in watts, and depth of discharge (DoD). This calculator applies the standard formula:

**Runtime (hours) = (Capacity in Ah × Voltage × Efficiency × DoD%) / Power in Watts**

The calculator validates your inputs against typical ranges and alerts you to values that might indicate an error.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference only. For critical decisions (financial, safety-critical systems, or specialized applications), consult a qualified specialist. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: Why can't I use 100% depth of discharge?
faq.a: Fully discharging a battery severely reduces its lifespan. Lead-acid batteries sulfate if completely discharged, losing capacity permanently. Always respect the recommended DoD limits for your battery type.

faq.q: Should I switch from lead-acid to lithium?
faq.a: LiFePO4 costs 2–3× more upfront but lasts 5–10× longer and weighs 40% less. Solar, RV, and marine installations increasingly choose lithium for lower lifetime cost despite higher initial investment.

faq.q: How do I calculate runtime with varying loads?
faq.a: Sum the watt-hours (W × hours) for each device, then divide by your battery's total usable energy (Wh). For complex variable loads, simulate hour-by-hour or use a power management system.

faq.q: Do batteries lose capacity over time?
faq.a: Yes. Li-ion loses ~2–3% per year plus degradation per cycle. LiFePO4 loses 1–2% per year. Lead-acid loses 5–10% per year depending on usage and maintenance.
Fast discharge reduces effective capacity. It matters if you drain your battery in under 10 hours. For longer discharge times, the effect is minimal.
Yes, all our calculators are free and require no registration.
Results are indicative, using internationally validated formulas. For mission-critical applications, consult a professional engineer or battery specialist.
No. All calculations happen in your browser. We never send data to any external server.

faq.q: How do I convert amperage to watts?
faq.a: Use P (watts) = A (amperes) × V (voltage). For example, a 10A draw at 12V = 120W. Convert to watts before entering into the calculator.

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
